Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: first I need to mentally map out the standard Hire to Retire sequence job posting candidate screening interviews hiring decision onboarding payroll setup case closure The key logical dependencies are that screening must happen before interviews interviews before decision and onboarding payroll closure must happen in order post decision

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Looking at Model 1 I notice immediately that the partial order has a critical issue Screen Decide and Screen Interview edges create parallel paths where Decide can happen without Interview ever occurring

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That is a severe logical flaw how can you make a hiring decision without conducting interviews

Name: Validation - IND
Text: The rest of the sequence seems fine otherwise

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Model 2 uses operators more interestingly The loop on onboarding is odd why repeat onboarding but not necessarily wrong The XOR for payroll is problematic though since skipping payroll entirely violates legal practical requirements

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: However the core sequence up to the decision point is actually correct here unlike Model 1

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Weighing the anomalies Model 1 missing interview path is a fundamental process integrity issue while Model 2 issues are more about excessive flexibility or optional steps

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So Model 2 is closer to normative despite its quirks

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
